subject: Physics
title: Van de Graaff Generators
description: A quick win: understand Van de Graaff Generators.

hook.text: Touch a Van de Graaff generator and your hair stands straight up. It's charging you with millions of volts.

buildup.text: A moving belt carries charges to a hollow metal sphere. The charges accumulate on the outside surface.

discovery.text: The sphere can reach millions of volts. When you touch it, the charge spreads across your body and hair.

twist.text: Each hair gets the same charge and repels its neighbors—so they all push apart and stand on end.

climax.text: Robert Van de Graaff built the original in 1929 to accelerate particles for nuclear physics research.

punchline.text: Millions of volts. Hair rebellion. All because like charges repel.

quiz.question: Why does your hair stand up when touching a Van de Graaff generator?
options: Each hair gets the same charge and like charges repel each other
The static electricity makes hair lighter
The generator creates a strong wind that lifts hair
